title: A Scotchman Whose Name Was Isbister
content:
A Scotchman whose name was Isbister
    Had a maiden giraffe he called �sister�
    When she said �Oh, be mine,
    Be my sweet Valentine!�
    He just shinned up her long neck and kissed her.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ellis Parker Butler, titled "A Scotchman Whose Name Was Isbister", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
